# Source Card: Comparison of different equations for estimating the glomerular filtration rate in pediatric kidney transplant recipients

## Summary
This peer-reviewed cross-sectional study (Siriraj Hospital, Thailand; PMID 40983736, DOI 10.1007/s00467-025-06942-8) compared 12 plasma-creatinine-based eGFR equations against the CKiD 2012 combined creatinine-cystatin C equation as reference in 23 pediatric kidney transplant recipients (120 samples) who received adult-donor kidneys. For the pediatric CDS module it establishes (a) which pediatric eGFR equations best agree with the combined-marker reference in this transplant population, and (b) equation-specific eGFR *agreement* thresholds (~70 and ~60 mL/min/1.73 m2) below which the creatinine-only equations track the reference and above which bias increases. The reference is an equation, not measured GFR, so all figures are method-comparison agreement statistics, not accuracy against a gold standard.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) ROC-identified agreement thresholds: <70 mL/min/1.73 m2 for Schwartz-Lyon, U25, FAS-height; <60 for Bedside Schwartz — locator: Results ROC paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:191a29759aec23ceef7aa74d07559f1c405174fc174a322447f43fc410d1ac82]"
- (ev_002) Schwartz-Lyon highest PCr-only agreement (ICC 0.913, CCC 0.911, TDI 14.0, P30 99.2%) — locator: Results formula-performance paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b678350d6e151bb40c43279015ff82ffdcf132e95bb4515dbe4cd8f3757b1fb0]"
- (ev_003) U25 combined PCr-CystC vs CKiD 2012 reference (ICC 0.993, CCC 0.990, TDI 3.9, P30 100%) — locator: Results U25-correlation paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d38f481c265d35af739205e3e8ea951468861852956d7545802a8d64d8a0308f]"
- (ev_004) PCr-only U25 / FAS-height / Bedside Schwartz comparable performance — locator: Results formula-performance paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:1c524ea6f7435aca77021d64e1738b6a91c7f23b1dd87afad8856863ed35b54b]"
- (ev_005) Assay-method dependence (PCr enzymatic; CystC particle-enhanced immunoturbidimetric) — locator: Methods measurement paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a9da198273ef84e2b572f5b3bee4f2d5d9b524418b6e6d2cc7708d6b20fce966]"
- (ev_006) Cohort: 120 samples / 23 recipients (mean age 14.2 +/- 3.4 y), adult donors — locator: Results demographics paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:904cf2091d3b64c1de10f56840d1112744842232bb55dbc5ec995c5ed0068da2]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: pediatric kidney transplant recipients aged 1-18 years receiving ADULT-donor kidneys; agreement was consistently better in the 10-18 year subgroup and at eGFR <75 mL/min/1.73 m2. No sex-specific thresholds reported. Neonatal/infant thresholds not addressed (only 1-18 y band).
- Assay dependence: PCr enzymatic method; CystC particle-enhanced immunoturbidimetric assay. All equation performance is conditional on these assay standardizations; thresholds are not transferable to Jaffe creatinine or unstandardized CystC without re-validation.
- Lifecycle: published 2025-09; no guideline version, no retire/supersede date. This is a single primary study, not a society guideline.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The ~70 and ~60 mL/min/1.73 m2 values are equation-vs-equation AGREEMENT thresholds (points beyond which creatinine-only eGFR diverges from the combined-marker reference), NOT clinical referral triggers, dialysis thresholds, or diagnostic cutoffs. They should be surfaced to the CDS converter only as "equation selection / reliability boundary" metadata, never as a patient-facing scope-exit or action trigger.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Small single-center cohort (n=23 patients, 120 samples); no measured-GFR gold standard — all statistics are equation-vs-equation agreement, so "accuracy" is relative to the CKiD 2012 combined equation only.
- Reference CKiD 2012 equation was derived in children with mostly CKD stage >=3; the source notes it "may not fully reflect transplant recipients with near-normal GFR," and accuracy fell at higher eGFR — a conflict the source preserves rather than resolves.
- Population-specific finding: FAS-height outperformed FAS-age here (attributed to Thai post-transplant growth retardation), which the source explicitly contrasts with the Canadian Alkandari et al. cohort where FAS-age performed better — conflicting equation rankings across populations are preserved, not averaged.
- GAP: the exact CKiD 2012, U25, Schwartz-Lyon, Bedside Schwartz, and FAS equation coefficients are referenced ("The equations used were as follows:") but the equation table did not render in the retrieved full text; no verbatim coefficient passage is available, so no equation constants are asserted here.
- GAP: superscript "2" in "mL/min/1.73 m2" is stripped in the retrieved PMC text (renders as "1.73 m"); the intended unit is mL/min/1.73 m^2 (UCUM mL/min/{1.73_m2}), confirmed by the article title/abstract context and standard eGFR reporting.
